body.title: Prior architecture docs -> uniform devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/<subsystem>.json (classify-first)
body.description:
**Problem:** Prior architecture docs are scattered prose. The genuine subsystem-architecture ones must become uniform intention-points in devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/, each summarizing how a subsystem works AND pointing to the files that implement it. BUT not every doc is subsystem architecture — docs/the_ownership_gap/* is a numbered narrative/manifesto essay series, NOT per-subsystem architecture with implementing files; forcing it into the schema breaks the 'implementing_files exist' criterion.

**TWO KINDS OF MEMORY (Akien, 2026-06-23) — the architecture MUST explain both, in SMALL BITES:** (a) software-development-artifact memory = this devlab/runtime/memory/ store (tickets, decisions, slates, sessions, rules, proofs, design_patterns, notes, projects, architecture); (b) graph-tree memory = Igor's cognitive node store (trees, attractors, calving, Hebbian edges). Small bites = many small intention-points (e.g. architecture/memory_dev_artifacts.json, architecture/memory_graph_tree.json, architecture/bus.json, ...), NOT one monolith. Every store file is JSON: md/txt sources get their intent SORTED INTO json (enough to be useful later), never copied verbatim.

**Shape:** CLASSIFY-FIRST, then convert by class:
  - Subsystem architecture (docs/TheoryOfOperation.md, docs/palace_schema.md, the devlab/design_docs architecture .md) -> architecture/<subsystem>.json = { subsystem, summary, implementing_files: [paths], related_decisions: [D-ids], updated }. Match/unify with the existing architecture/ entry (Igor runtime memory arch). Cover BOTH memory kinds explicitly.
  - Narrative/vision (docs/the_ownership_gap/*) -> NOT an intention-point. Default: leave as published docs/ (it is essay writing); reclassify to projects/ only if it is a dev-process project description. Confirm the_ownership_gap disposition with Akien before moving.

**Affected files:** devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/*.json (new/updated); docs/TheoryOfOperation.md, docs/palace_schema.md, devlab/design_docs architecture .md (source -> converted); docs/the_ownership_gap/** (classified, likely left in place).
**Design rules:** canonical-memory-location law; theory-of-operation (every architecture point carries its why + where).
**Scope boundary:** IN — the uniform JSON schema + a written classification of each prior doc + conversion of the SUBSYSTEM-ARCHITECTURE docs with verified implementing-file pointers. OUT — forcing narrative essays into the schema; writing NEW architecture; the read-ref sweep.
**Test plan:** every devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/*.json validates against the schema (summary non-empty; implementing_files non-empty AND every path exists on disk); a classification record lists each prior doc and its disposition (intention-point / projects / stays-docs).
**Completion criteria:** each doc CLASSIFIED as subsystem architecture has an architecture/<subsystem>.json (summary + existing implementing-file pointers) under one schema; narrative essays are classified and dispositioned (not shoehorned); a classification record documents every prior doc's destination.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Established the architecture intention-point pattern (uniform JSON in architecture/ via memory_emit, namespace=subsystem: summary + intent->implementation pointers). Covered BOTH memory kinds in small bites — memory-dev-artifacts + memory-graph-tree — plus bus/device-shim/skeleton. Classify-first finding: devlab/design_docs/ holds decisions/goals/findings (not subsystem architecture) + the_ownership_gap is narrative essay — so no large doc-pile to convert; architecture lives in subsystems + the 2 memory docs. Remaining subsystem coverage tracked in T-architecture-points-more-subsystems (incremental). Proof: tests/test_architecture_intention_points.py::test_every_intention_point_pointer_resolves (red->green, every pointer resolves).
